description: The test at FeedRow.dom.vitest.ts:878 incorrectly types the queried element as 'HTMLButtonElement | null' when the element is now a <div> (HTMLDivElement) after the Sprint 3 replacement of <button> with <div role='button'>. While this does not cause a runtime error (both have click()), it indicates the test was merely updated with a new data-testid selector but the type annotation was not updated to reflect the changed element type. This suggests the test is testing the data-testid presence rather than the structural change from <button> to <div>, making it a tautological test.

additionalEvidence.description: Test 'row uses div[role="button"] element (NFR-001)' does verify tagName and role, but the other tests still carry stale HTMLButtonElement type assertions

remediationHint: Update all type assertions in FeedRow.dom.vitest.ts from 'HTMLButtonElement' to 'HTMLDivElement' (or use 'HTMLElement') to accurately reflect the div[role='button'] element type, and add assertions for the div's onkeydown behavior.
